October 2016 |location=London}}{{cite news |title=Million Pound Game: Hull KR 18-19 Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37488691 |access-date=4 November 2024 |work=BBC Sport |date=1 October 2016}} |} This match resulted in Hull Kingston Rovers' [[relegation]] from the Super League after ten consecutive years into the [[RFL Championship]]. Following [[Gareth O'Brien]]'s match-winning [[drop goal]], a [[pitch invasion]] took place that resulted in fighting between fans and stewards that eventually required police intervention. Following a [[RFL]] investigation, in February 2017, Hull Kingston Rovers were fined £25,000 (£20,000 suspended) after the RFL found the club guilty of breaking ground safety rules and failing to control their supporters.{{cite news |title=Hull KR and Salford fined for Million Pound Game crowd disturbance |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/10778526/hull-kr-and-salford-fined-for-million-pound-game-crowd-disturbance |access-date=4 November 2024 |work=Sky Sports |date=23 February 2017}} ===Table=== {{2016 Rugby League Qualifiers table|showteam=HKR}} ==Challenge Cup== {{main|2016 Challenge Cup}} {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Round!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!Report |- | 16 April; 14:00 | Round 5 | {{leagueicon|Oldham|16}} [[Oldham Roughyeds]] | H | [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] | {{WinDL|res=L}} | 22–36 | [[Dane Tilse|Tilse]] (2), [[Shaun Lunt|Lunt]], [[Maurice Blair|Blair]], [[Joe Wardill|Wardill]] | [[Ben Cockayne|Cockayne]] (1/4) | 3,056 | {{cite news |title=Hull KR 22-36 Oldham Roughyeds: Championship side stun Rovers |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/hull-k-r-vs-oldham/45077 |access-date=4 November 2024 |work=Sky Sports |date=16 April 2016}} |} ==Transfers== ===Gains=== {| class="wikitable" |- !
